title: lilium longiflorum subsp. neilgherrense
intro: Lilium longiflorum subsp. neilgherrense, also known as the Neilgherries lily, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Easter lily plant that is native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant's flowers have been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and bulbs are traditionally used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
